title: picea excelsa var. obovata
intro:
Picea excelsa var. obovata, also known as Korean spruce or Korean fir, is a type of evergreen coniferous tree native to Northeast Asia, characterized by its narrow, obovate leaves and conical shape.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as pinene, limonene, and bornyl acetate, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.

The health benefits of Picea excelsa var. obovata include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system, while also being used to treat various ailments such as bronchitis, asthma, and skin conditions.

In herbal preparations, Picea excelsa var. obovata is often used in the form of essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested in moderation, under the guidance of a healthcare professional, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
